metadata.title: Prolonged fasting-induced metabolic signatures in human skeletal muscle of lean and obese men
metadata.study_design: Experimental study comparing lean and obese participants during fasting periods
metadata.demographics: 18 apparently healthy young men aged from 20 to 35 years; nine lean (BMI 19–23) and nine obese (BMI 32–40 kg/m²)

evidence.quote: Prolonged fasting-induced metabolic signatures in human skeletal muscle of lean and obese men
18 apparently healthy young men aged from 20 to 35 years participated; nine lean (BMI 19–23) and nine obese (BMI 32–40 kg/m²)
we examined nine obese and nine lean participants during 12 and 72 h of fasting, respectively

methods_participants.label: Study Design
methods_participants.value: Randomized crossover design with two study occasions separated by minimum 21 days

narrative_results.statement: In obese participants, the levels of the TCA-cycle intermediates citrate, succinate, and malate were reduced compared to lean independently of the duration of the fast
Found very high levels of β-OHB in the fasting muscle and a concomitant increase in β-OHB-carnitine, demonstrating that ketone bodies were metabolized in skeletal muscle
The metabolic adaptation to prolonged fasting involving changes in glycogen breakdown are lost in muscle from insulin resistant obese participants
Myocellular energetics were preserved and there were no suggestions of deficient substrate oxidation and obvious mitochondrial defects despite insulin resistance
The metabolic signatures in human skeletal muscle during acute and chronic insulin resistance reveal distinct differences
